title: Long Presumed Dead, a West African Reef Is Found Thriving
excerpt: This article was originally published by Inside Climate News and is reproduced here as part of the Climate Desk collaboration.
content_html:
This article was originally published by Inside Climate News and is reproduced here as part of the Climate Desk collaboration.

Sixty years after surveyors first uncovered evidence of a sprawling coral reef off the West African nation of Benin, scientists have at last found the reef.

In the 1960s, fishing surveyors off West Africa’s Benin coast hauled up heads of coral in their nets. Employed by local governments to assess fish diversity and discover potentially trawlable seabeds in the sandy-bottomed Gulf of Guinea, the researchers shrugged off the discovery at the time, burying the find in a brief paragraph in a 130-something-page report.

With no surveys since, the scientists who followed had lost track of exactly where the reefs might lie. And as mass bleaching events and overfishing slashed the world’s coral reef area by more than 50 percent since the 1950s, local oceanographers had written off any remnants of a possible reef as dead.

More than six decades on, the mystery has been solved as a team of Beninese scientists rediscovered the healthy reef teeming with marine life. At least eight coral types and eight fish species have formed a thriving ecosystem on this long-forgotten site. 

“I was guided by hope. A hope that sometimes there are species or ecosystems which kind of defeat time, or are trying to resist it,” said Gérard Zinzindohoué, the project lead for Coral Reefs Rediscovering & Exploration in Benin.

Fresh off seeing coral for the first time in Cape Verde in 2021, Zinzindohoué asked himself a simple question: Do we have any reefs in Benin?

After a colleague passed on the original 1960s report — which mapped out a potential 24-mile-long coral reef barrier along Benin’s coastline — Zinzindohoué recognized a gap in the scientific knowledge: “I realized how little we know about our own coastal environment.” 

But in the years that followed, a string of failed grant applications halted progress on solving the mystery.

When he secured a $20,000 National Geographic Explorers grant in January 2025, the project finally ignited. Yet getting the gear was also challenging. The sonar equipment swallowed up almost 80 percent of the grant budget and nearly stalled entirely when the European supplier refused to accept payment from Zinzindohoué’s African bank account.

To complicate matters further, Benin doesn’t have a permanent research vessel. Zinzindohoué therefore had no choice but to seek the help of local fishermen to ferry the team to the suspected site 14 miles offshore and jury-rig the pirogue to accommodate towing the high-tech sonar device.

“The fishermen’s boats are not used to going that far offshore, so it was risky for everyone,” said Zinzindohoué, whose team battled repeated engine failures and months of nausea-filled fieldwork before finally locating two strong sonar echoes pinging from the depths below. National Geographic — whom Zinzindohoué credits with the success of the project — then dispatched a high-resolution deep-sea camera system from their Exploration Technology Lab to investigate the sonar results.

While their wooden pirogue bounced atop the offshore swell, the team filmed the seafloor below but had to wait until they returned to dry land to scour the footage. What they had captured was a wealth of marine life: six types of soft coral; two black corals; and eight species of sheltering fish, from golden African snappers to the Monrovia doctorfish.

When the first images came through, Zinzindohoué texted Houangninan Midinoudewa, an oceanographic researcher at the Benin Marine Conservation Club and the same friend who first told Zinzindohoué of the 1960s report.

Midinoudewa remembers the moment: “I said, ‘Bro, 60 years later we are now discovering exactly what we have in our water.’ We found it still alive, producing more fish for communities and supporting livelihoods. I was really joyful and really overwhelmed.”

While no coral samples have yet been extracted, researchers have classified the site as a mesophotic coral ecosystem (MCE). Such systems are light-dependent communities existing at the lower limits of reef-building corals. At more than 175 feet below the surface, the coral garden they discovered appears patchily scattered on a rocky substrate.

Bridging the gap between shallow reefs and deeper benthic habitats, MCEs are home to distinct species and unique environmental conditions. While scientists are increasingly recognizing their ecological and climatic importance, they remain among the least explored elements of tropical and subtropical marine biodiversity. And this one has real potential to unlock new information about coral history.

“Since it’s an undisturbed marine ecosystem, it can help through carbon dating or paleoclimatic study to tell us which kind of climate system has occurred here in the past,” said Zinzindohoué. “It’s better to know the past to help explain the present, and help know which kind of direction we can take in the future.”

In addition to the blackbar soldierfish, West African goatfish, and Guinean angelfish filmed darting through the reef, the discovery presents potential conservation claims for other marine life.

“We will be advocating for its full protection, perhaps by setting up a marine protected area around it,” said Midinoudewa, who specializes in elasmobranchs — the study of sharks, rays, and skates.

Midinoudewa is currently submitting an application to designate the area as an Important Shark and Ray Area with the International Union for Conservation of Nature. Based on the knowledge of local fishermen, Midinoudewa is confident the reef is home to sawback angel sharks, silky sharks, brown skates, and marbled stingrays. 

The team behind the discovery hopes this will spark a wave of similar discoveries in the waters off West Africa, an area of the world underserved by scientific research projects 

“I hope the Gulf of Guinea will be a hub for research because we know our resources are being exploited and people [need to] know exactly what we have and why we should care,” said Midinoudewa. 

Zinzindohoué agrees: “We don’t need to wait for others to come to our country to show us what is under our sea. We are the ones who must take responsibility.”

—Johnny Sturgeon, Inside Climate News

sentences.zh: 20 世纪 60 年代，西非贝宁海岸的渔业测量员在网中打捞出珊瑚头。

sentences.en: Employed by local governments to assess fish diversity and discover potentially trawlable seabeds in the sandy-bottomed Gulf of Guinea, the researchers shrugged off the discovery at the time, burying the find in a brief paragraph in a 130-something-page report.
sentences.zh: 研究人员受地方政府雇佣，负责评估几内亚湾沙底鱼类多样性并发现潜在的可拖网海底，但研究人员当时对这一发现不屑一顾，将这一发现埋在了一份 130 多页报告中的一个简短段落中。

blocks.html_en: With no surveys since, the scientists who followed had lost track of exactly where the reefs might lie. And as mass bleaching events and overfishing slashed the world’s coral reef area by more than 50 percent since the 1950s, local oceanographers had written off any remnants of a possible reef as dead.

sentences.zh: 此后，由于没有进行任何调查，后续的科学家们已经不知道珊瑚礁可能位于何处。

sentences.en: And as mass bleaching events and overfishing slashed the world’s coral reef area by more than 50 percent since the 1950s, local oceanographers had written off any remnants of a possible reef as dead.
sentences.zh: 自 20 世纪 50 年代以来，大规模白化事件和过度捕捞使世界珊瑚礁面积减少了 50% 以上，当地海洋学家将任何可能存在的珊瑚礁残余物视为已死亡。

blocks.html_en: More than six decades on, the mystery has been solved as a team of Beninese scientists rediscovered the healthy reef teeming with marine life. At least eight coral types and eight fish species have formed a thriving ecosystem on this long-forgotten site.

sentences.en: More than six decades on, the mystery has been solved as a team of Beninese scientists rediscovered the healthy reef teeming with marine life.
sentences.zh: 六年多过去了，贝宁科学家团队重新发现了充满海洋生物的健康珊瑚礁，这个谜团得以解开。

sentences.en: At least eight coral types and eight fish species have formed a thriving ecosystem on this long-forgotten site.
sentences.zh: 至少八种珊瑚类型和八种鱼类在这个长期被遗忘的地点形成了繁荣的生态系统。

sentences.en: A hope that sometimes there are species or ecosystems which kind of defeat time, or are trying to resist it,” said Gérard Zinzindohoué, the project lead for Coral Reefs Rediscovering & Exploration in Benin.
sentences.zh: 希望有时有些物种或生态系统会打败时间，或者试图抵抗时间，”贝宁珊瑚礁重新发现和探索项目负责人热拉尔·津津多乌埃 (Gérard Zinzindohoué) 说。

blocks.html_en: Fresh off seeing coral for the first time in Cape Verde in 2021, Zinzindohoué asked himself a simple question: Do we have any reefs in Benin?

sentences.zh: 2021 年，Zinzindohoué 第一次在佛得角看到珊瑚，他问自己一个简单的问题：贝宁有珊瑚礁吗？

blocks.html_en: After a colleague passed on the original 1960s report — which mapped out a potential 24-mile-long coral reef barrier along Benin’s coastline — Zinzindohoué recognized a gap in the scientific knowledge: “I realized how little we know about our own coastal environment.”

sentences.zh: 在一位同事传递了 1960 年代的原始报告（该报告绘制了贝宁海岸线上潜在的 24 英里长的珊瑚礁屏障）后，Zinzindohoué 认识到了科学知识中的差距：“我意识到我们对自己的沿海环境知之甚少。”

blocks.html_en: But in the years that followed, a string of failed grant applications halted progress on solving the mystery.

sentences.en: But in the years that followed, a string of failed grant applications halted progress on solving the mystery.
sentences.zh: 但在接下来的几年里，一连串失败的拨款申请阻碍了解决这个谜团的进展。

blocks.html_en: When he secured a $20,000 National Geographic Explorers grant in January 2025, the project finally ignited. Yet getting the gear was also challenging. The sonar equipment swallowed up almost 80 percent of the grant budget and nearly stalled entirely when the European supplier refused to accept payment from Zinzindohoué’s African bank account.

sentences.en: When he secured a $20,000 National Geographic Explorers grant in January 2025, the project finally ignited.
sentences.zh: 当他于 2025 年 1 月获得国家地理探险家 20,000 美元的资助时，该项目终于启动。

sentences.en: Yet getting the gear was also challenging.
sentences.zh: 然而获得装备也充满挑战。

sentences.en: The sonar equipment swallowed up almost 80 percent of the grant budget and nearly stalled entirely when the European supplier refused to accept payment from Zinzindohoué’s African bank account.
sentences.zh: 声纳设备消耗了近 80% 的拨款预算，并且当欧洲供应商拒绝接受 Zinzindohoué 非洲银行账户的付款时，声纳设备几乎完全陷入停滞。

blocks.html_en: To complicate matters further, Benin doesn’t have a permanent research vessel. Zinzindohoué therefore had no choice but to seek the help of local fishermen to ferry the team to the suspected site 14 miles offshore and jury-rig the pirogue to accommodate towing the high-tech sonar device.

sentences.en: To complicate matters further, Benin doesn’t have a permanent research vessel.
sentences.zh: 更复杂的是，贝宁没有永久性的研究船。

sentences.en: Zinzindohoué therefore had no choice but to seek the help of local fishermen to ferry the team to the suspected site 14 miles offshore and jury-rig the pirogue to accommodate towing the high-tech sonar device.
sentences.zh: 因此，津津多乌埃别无选择，只能寻求当地渔民的帮助，将团队运送到离岸 14 英里的可疑地点，并临时装备独木舟以拖曳高科技声纳装置。

blocks.html_en: “The fishermen’s boats are not used to going that far offshore, so it was risky for everyone,” said Zinzindohoué, whose team battled repeated engine failures and months of nausea-filled fieldwork before finally locating two strong sonar echoes pinging from the depths below. National Geographic — whom Zinzindohoué credits with the success of the project — then dispatched a high-resolution deep-sea camera system from their Exploration Technology Lab to investigate the sonar results.

sentences.en: “The fishermen’s boats are not used to going that far offshore, so it was risky for everyone,” said Zinzindohoué, whose team battled repeated engine failures and months of nausea-filled fieldwork before finally locating two strong sonar echoes pinging from the depths below.
sentences.zh: Zinzindohoué 说：“渔民的船不习惯离岸那么远，所以这对每个人来说都是危险的。”他的团队经历了多次发动机故障和数月令人恶心的实地工作，最终找到了从海底深处发出的两个强烈声纳回声。

sentences.en: National Geographic — whom Zinzindohoué credits with the success of the project — then dispatched a high-resolution deep-sea camera system from their Exploration Technology Lab to investigate the sonar results.
sentences.zh: 国家地理杂志——津津多乌埃认为该项目的成功归功于该杂志——随后从其勘探技术实验室派遣了高分辨率深海摄像系统来调查声纳结果。

What they had captured was a wealth of marine life: six types of soft coral; two black corals; and eight species of sheltering fish, from golden African snappers to the Monrovia doctorfish.

sentences.en: While their wooden pirogue bounced atop the offshore swell, the team filmed the seafloor below but had to wait until they returned to dry land to scour the footage.
sentences.zh: 当他们的木独木舟在离岸海浪上弹跳时，团队拍摄了下面的海底，但必须等到他们返回陆地才能清理镜头。

sentences.en: What they had captured was a wealth of marine life: six types of soft coral; two black corals; and eight species of sheltering fish, from golden African snappers to the Monrovia doctorfish.
sentences.zh: 他们捕获的是丰富的海洋生物：六种软珊瑚；两个黑珊瑚；以及八种庇护鱼，从非洲金鲷鱼到蒙罗维亚医生鱼。

blocks.html_en: When the first images came through, Zinzindohoué texted Houangninan Midinoudewa, an oceanographic researcher at the Benin Marine Conservation Club and the same friend who first told Zinzindohoué of the 1960s report.

sentences.en: When the first images came through, Zinzindohoué texted Houangninan Midinoudewa, an oceanographic researcher at the Benin Marine Conservation Club and the same friend who first told Zinzindohoué of the 1960s report.
sentences.zh: 当第一批图像发布后，Zinzindohoué 给贝宁海洋保护俱乐部的海洋学研究员 Houangninan Midinoudewa 发了短信，他也是第一个告诉 Zinzindohoué 1960 年代报告的朋友。

blocks.html_en: Midinoudewa remembers the moment: “I said, ‘Bro, 60 years later we are now discovering exactly what we have in our water.’ We found it still alive, producing more fish for communities and supporting livelihoods. I was really joyful and really overwhelmed.”

sentences.en: Midinoudewa remembers the moment: “I said, ‘Bro, 60 years later we are now discovering exactly what we have in our water.’
sentences.zh: Midinoudewa 记得那一刻：“我说，‘兄弟，60 年后，我们现在确切地发现了水中的成分。’”

sentences.en: We found it still alive, producing more fish for communities and supporting livelihoods.
sentences.zh: 我们发现它仍然活着，为社区生产更多的鱼并支持生计。

sentences.en: I was really joyful and really overwhelmed.”
sentences.zh: 我真的很高兴，也真的不知所措。”

blocks.html_en: While no coral samples have yet been extracted, researchers have classified the site as a mesophotic coral ecosystem (MCE). Such systems are light-dependent communities existing at the lower limits of reef-building corals. At more than 175 feet below the surface, the coral garden they discovered appears patchily scattered on a rocky substrate.

sentences.en: While no coral samples have yet been extracted, researchers have classified the site as a mesophotic coral ecosystem (MCE).
sentences.zh: 虽然尚未提取珊瑚样本，但研究人员已将该地点归类为中光珊瑚生态系统（MCE）。

sentences.en: Such systems are light-dependent communities existing at the lower limits of reef-building corals.
sentences.zh: 这些系统是存在于造礁珊瑚下限的依赖光的群落。

sentences.en: At more than 175 feet below the surface, the coral garden they discovered appears patchily scattered on a rocky substrate.
sentences.zh: 他们发现的珊瑚花园位于地表以下超过 175 英尺的地方，零散地散布在岩石基底上。

blocks.html_en: Bridging the gap between shallow reefs and deeper benthic habitats, MCEs are home to distinct species and unique environmental conditions. While scientists are increasingly recognizing their ecological and climatic importance, they remain among the least explored elements of tropical and subtropical marine biodiversity. And this one has real potential to unlock new information about coral history.

sentences.en: Bridging the gap between shallow reefs and deeper benthic habitats, MCEs are home to distinct species and unique environmental conditions.
sentences.zh: MCE 弥合了浅层珊瑚礁和较深的底栖栖息地之间的差距，是独特物种和独特环境条件的家园。

sentences.en: While scientists are increasingly recognizing their ecological and climatic importance, they remain among the least explored elements of tropical and subtropical marine biodiversity.
sentences.zh: 尽管科学家们越来越认识到它们对生态和气候的重要性，但它们仍然是热带和亚热带海洋生物多样性中探索最少的元素之一。

sentences.en: And this one has real potential to unlock new information about coral history.
sentences.zh: 这确实有潜力解开有关珊瑚历史的新信息。

blocks.html_en: “Since it’s an undisturbed marine ecosystem, it can help through carbon dating or paleoclimatic study to tell us which kind of climate system has occurred here in the past,” said Zinzindohoué. “It’s better to know the past to help explain the present, and help know which kind of direction we can take in the future.”

sentences.en: “Since it’s an undisturbed marine ecosystem, it can help through carbon dating or paleoclimatic study to tell us which kind of climate system has occurred here in the past,” said Zinzindohoué.
sentences.zh: “由于它是一个未受干扰的海洋生态系统，因此可以通过碳测年或古气候研究来帮助我们了解过去这里发生过哪种气候系统，”Zinzindohoué 说。

sentences.en: “It’s better to know the past to help explain the present, and help know which kind of direction we can take in the future.”
sentences.zh: “了解过去有助于解释现在，并有助于了解我们未来可以采取的方向。”

blocks.html_en: In addition to the blackbar soldierfish, West African goatfish, and Guinean angelfish filmed darting through the reef, the discovery presents potential conservation claims for other marine life.

sentences.en: In addition to the blackbar soldierfish, West African goatfish, and Guinean angelfish filmed darting through the reef, the discovery presents potential conservation claims for other marine life.
sentences.zh: 除了拍摄到的黑条士兵鱼、西非山羊鱼和几内亚神仙鱼在珊瑚礁中穿梭的情况外，这一发现还为其他海洋生物提出了潜在的保护主张。

blocks.html_en: “We will be advocating for its full protection, perhaps by setting up a marine protected area around it,” said Midinoudewa, who specializes in elasmobranchs — the study of sharks, rays, and skates.

sentences.en: “We will be advocating for its full protection, perhaps by setting up a marine protected area around it,” said Midinoudewa, who specializes in elasmobranchs — the study of sharks, rays, and skates.
sentences.zh: “我们将倡导对其进行全面保护，或许可以在它周围建立一个海洋保护区，”专门研究软骨鱼类（研究鲨鱼、鳐鱼和鳐鱼）的米迪努德瓦说。

blocks.html_en: Midinoudewa is currently submitting an application to designate the area as an Important Shark and Ray Area with the International Union for Conservation of Nature. Based on the knowledge of local fishermen, Midinoudewa is confident the reef is home to sawback angel sharks, silky sharks, brown skates, and marbled stingrays.

sentences.en: Midinoudewa is currently submitting an application to designate the area as an Important Shark and Ray Area with the International Union for Conservation of Nature.
sentences.zh: 米迪诺德瓦目前正在向国际自然保护联盟提交申请，将该地区指定为重要的鲨鱼和鳐鱼区。

sentences.en: Based on the knowledge of local fishermen, Midinoudewa is confident the reef is home to sawback angel sharks, silky sharks, brown skates, and marbled stingrays.
sentences.zh: 根据当地渔民的了解，米迪努德瓦确信该礁石是锯背天使鲨、丝鲨、棕鳐鱼和大理石黄貂鱼的家园。

blocks.html_en: The team behind the discovery hopes this will spark a wave of similar discoveries in the waters off West Africa, an area of the world underserved by scientific research projects

sentences.en: The team behind the discovery hopes this will spark a wave of similar discoveries in the waters off West Africa, an area of the world underserved by scientific research projects
sentences.zh: 这一发现背后的团队希望这将在西非海域引发一波类似的发现浪潮，该地区是世界上科学研究项目服务不足的地区

blocks.html_en: “I hope the Gulf of Guinea will be a hub for research because we know our resources are being exploited and people [need to] know exactly what we have and why we should care,” said Midinoudewa.

sentences.en: “I hope the Gulf of Guinea will be a hub for research because we know our resources are being exploited and people [need to] know exactly what we have and why we should care,” said Midinoudewa.
sentences.zh: “我希望几内亚湾将成为研究中心，因为我们知道我们的资源正在被开发，人们[需要]确切地知道我们拥有什么以及为什么我们应该关心，”米迪努德瓦说。

blocks.html_en: Zinzindohoué agrees: “We don’t need to wait for others to come to our country to show us what is under our sea. We are the ones who must take responsibility.”

sentences.en: Zinzindohoué agrees: “We don’t need to wait for others to come to our country to show us what is under our sea.
sentences.zh: Zinzydohoué 对此表示同意：“我们不需要等待其他人来到我们的国家向我们展示海底世界。

sentences.en: We are the ones who must take responsibility.”
sentences.zh: 我们是必须承担责任的人。”

blocks.html_en: —Johnny Sturgeon, Inside Climate News

sentences.en: —Johnny Sturgeon, Inside Climate News
sentences.zh: ——约翰尼·斯特金，《气候新闻内部》

content_html_zh:
本文最初由《内部气候新闻》发表，并作为气候台合作的一部分转载于此。

在测量员首次发现西非国家贝宁附近有一片广阔的珊瑚礁的证据六十年后，科学家们终于找到了珊瑚礁。

20 世纪 60 年代，西非贝宁海岸的渔业测量员在网中打捞出珊瑚头。 研究人员受地方政府雇佣，负责评估几内亚湾沙底鱼类多样性并发现潜在的可拖网海底，但研究人员当时对这一发现不屑一顾，将这一发现埋在了一份 130 多页报告中的一个简短段落中。

此后，由于没有进行任何调查，后续的科学家们已经不知道珊瑚礁可能位于何处。 自 20 世纪 50 年代以来，大规模白化事件和过度捕捞使世界珊瑚礁面积减少了 50% 以上，当地海洋学家将任何可能存在的珊瑚礁残余物视为已死亡。

六年多过去了，贝宁科学家团队重新发现了充满海洋生物的健康珊瑚礁，这个谜团得以解开。 至少八种珊瑚类型和八种鱼类在这个长期被遗忘的地点形成了繁荣的生态系统。

“我受到希望的指引。 希望有时有些物种或生态系统会打败时间，或者试图抵抗时间，”贝宁珊瑚礁重新发现和探索项目负责人热拉尔·津津多乌埃 (Gérard Zinzindohoué) 说。

2021 年，Zinzindohoué 第一次在佛得角看到珊瑚，他问自己一个简单的问题：贝宁有珊瑚礁吗？

在一位同事传递了 1960 年代的原始报告（该报告绘制了贝宁海岸线上潜在的 24 英里长的珊瑚礁屏障）后，Zinzindohoué 认识到了科学知识中的差距：“我意识到我们对自己的沿海环境知之甚少。”

但在接下来的几年里，一连串失败的拨款申请阻碍了解决这个谜团的进展。

当他于 2025 年 1 月获得国家地理探险家 20,000 美元的资助时，该项目终于启动。 然而获得装备也充满挑战。 声纳设备消耗了近 80% 的拨款预算，并且当欧洲供应商拒绝接受 Zinzindohoué 非洲银行账户的付款时，声纳设备几乎完全陷入停滞。

更复杂的是，贝宁没有永久性的研究船。 因此，津津多乌埃别无选择，只能寻求当地渔民的帮助，将团队运送到离岸 14 英里的可疑地点，并临时装备独木舟以拖曳高科技声纳装置。

Zinzindohoué 说：“渔民的船不习惯离岸那么远，所以这对每个人来说都是危险的。”他的团队经历了多次发动机故障和数月令人恶心的实地工作，最终找到了从海底深处发出的两个强烈声纳回声。 国家地理杂志——津津多乌埃认为该项目的成功归功于该杂志——随后从其勘探技术实验室派遣了高分辨率深海摄像系统来调查声纳结果。

当他们的木独木舟在离岸海浪上弹跳时，团队拍摄了下面的海底，但必须等到他们返回陆地才能清理镜头。 他们捕获的是丰富的海洋生物：六种软珊瑚；两个黑珊瑚；以及八种庇护鱼，从非洲金鲷鱼到蒙罗维亚医生鱼。

当第一批图像发布后，Zinzindohoué 给贝宁海洋保护俱乐部的海洋学研究员 Houangninan Midinoudewa 发了短信，他也是第一个告诉 Zinzindohoué 1960 年代报告的朋友。

Midinoudewa 记得那一刻：“我说，‘兄弟，60 年后，我们现在确切地发现了水中的成分。’” 我们发现它仍然活着，为社区生产更多的鱼并支持生计。 我真的很高兴，也真的不知所措。”

虽然尚未提取珊瑚样本，但研究人员已将该地点归类为中光珊瑚生态系统（MCE）。 这些系统是存在于造礁珊瑚下限的依赖光的群落。 他们发现的珊瑚花园位于地表以下超过 175 英尺的地方，零散地散布在岩石基底上。

MCE 弥合了浅层珊瑚礁和较深的底栖栖息地之间的差距，是独特物种和独特环境条件的家园。 尽管科学家们越来越认识到它们对生态和气候的重要性，但它们仍然是热带和亚热带海洋生物多样性中探索最少的元素之一。 这确实有潜力解开有关珊瑚历史的新信息。

“由于它是一个未受干扰的海洋生态系统，因此可以通过碳测年或古气候研究来帮助我们了解过去这里发生过哪种气候系统，”Zinzindohoué 说。 “了解过去有助于解释现在，并有助于了解我们未来可以采取的方向。”

除了拍摄到的黑条士兵鱼、西非山羊鱼和几内亚神仙鱼在珊瑚礁中穿梭的情况外，这一发现还为其他海洋生物提出了潜在的保护主张。

“我们将倡导对其进行全面保护，或许可以在它周围建立一个海洋保护区，”专门研究软骨鱼类（研究鲨鱼、鳐鱼和鳐鱼）的米迪努德瓦说。

米迪诺德瓦目前正在向国际自然保护联盟提交申请，将该地区指定为重要的鲨鱼和鳐鱼区。 根据当地渔民的了解，米迪努德瓦确信该礁石是锯背天使鲨、丝鲨、棕鳐鱼和大理石黄貂鱼的家园。

这一发现背后的团队希望这将在西非海域引发一波类似的发现浪潮，该地区是世界上科学研究项目服务不足的地区

“我希望几内亚湾将成为研究中心，因为我们知道我们的资源正在被开发，人们[需要]确切地知道我们拥有什么以及为什么我们应该关心，”米迪努德瓦说。

Zinzydohoué 对此表示同意：“我们不需要等待其他人来到我们的国家向我们展示海底世界。 我们是必须承担责任的人。”

——约翰尼·斯特金，《气候新闻内部》
